description.para: These handlers are used to determine the type of the interrupt handler being registered with the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem.
Since the subsystem is tightly coupled with the CSI Rx Controller driver, the handlers from the sub core are promoted to the subsystem level so that the application can use them.
Please refer to CSI driver for details of the bitmasks.
The application should use the XCSISS_ISR* masks in the call back functions to decode the exact cause of interrupt and handle it accordingly.
All interrupts are grouped into DPHY Level Errors, Protocol Decoding Errors, Packet Level Errors, Normal Errors, Frame Received interrupt and Short Packet related.
These are used by application to determine the exact event causing the interrupt

briefdescription.para: Callback type which acts as a wrapper on top of CSI Callback.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the event. For current core version, this parameter is "OR" of 0 or more XCSISS_ISR_*_MASK constants defined in xcsiss_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: Driver instance array of all sub-cores included in the design.

detaileddescription.para: This array holds the driver instances for all MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem instances in the system.

briefdescription.para: MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem configuration table (SDT)

detaileddescription.para: This table contains configuration information for each MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem instance in the system when using System Device Tree (SDT). Each entry describes the hardware configuration for a specific instance including sub-core presence and parameters.

briefdescription.para: MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem configuration table (SDT)

detaileddescription.para: This table contains configuration information for each MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem instance in the system when using System Device Tree (SDT). Each entry describes the hardware configuration for a specific instance including sub-core presence and parameters.

briefdescription.para: This function queries the subsystem instance configuration to determine the included sub-cores.

detaileddescription.para: For each sub-core that is present in the design the sub-core driver instance is binded with the subsystem sub-core driver handle.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the included sub-core to it's static configuration.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS If CSI sub core is initialised successfully.
XST_FAILURE If CSI sub core initialization fails.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the MIPI CSI subsystem and included sub-cores.

detaileddescription.para: Initialize the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem driver.

This function must be called prior to using the subsystem. Initialization includes setting up the instance data for top level as well as all included sub-core therein, and ensuring the hardware is in a known stable state.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
CfgPtr
points to the configuration structure associated with the subsystem instance.
EffectiveAddr
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.
XST_SUCCESS if initialization is successful.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.
None. This function initializes the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem driver. This function must be called prior to using the driver. Initialization includes setting up the instance data and ensuring the hardware is in a quiescent state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsiSs instance.
is a reference to a structure containing information about a specific XCsiSs instance.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to configure the CSI lanes and interrupts that are to be handled by the application.

detaileddescription.para: Configure the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem.
Refer to XCsi.h for interrupt masks.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
ActiveLanes
is no of active lanes to be configure. This value ranges between 1 and 4. In case Dynamic Active Lane config is enabled, this value can't exceed maximum lanes present. When Dynamic Active Lane config is disabled, it should be equal to maximum lanes.
IntrMask
Indicates Mask for enable interrupts.
XST_SUCCESS on successful configuration of parameters.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.
When EnableActiveLanes is 0, then the ActiveLanes parameter passed should be equal to Max Lanes of design. This function configures the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem with the specified active lanes and interrupt mask.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsiSs instance.
is the number of active lanes to be used.
is the interrupt mask to enable specific interrupts.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to activate the CSI Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: Activate or deactivate the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem.
Internally it activates the DPHY and CSI.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
Flag
is used to denote whether to enable or disable the subsystem.
XST_SUCCESS on successful operation.
XST_FAILURE on failed operation.
None. This function activates or deactivates the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem based on the Flag parameter.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsiSs instance.
is the enable/disable flag. TRUE to enable, FALSE to disable.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to reset the CSI Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: Reset the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem.
Internally it resets the DPHY and CSI only as the IIC instance is separately handled by the application.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
XST_SUCCESS if all the sub core IP resets occur correctly.
XST_FAILURE if reset fails for any sub core IP.
None. This function resets the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsiSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function reports list of cores included.

detaileddescription.para: Report the core information.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
None.
None. This function reports the core information of the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsiSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function will control the virtual channels selection dynamically.

detaileddescription.para: Set the virtual channel selection.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
Value
will set the virtual channels corresponding to each bit value.
None.
In order to use dynamic VC selection, 'Allowed VC to all' must be selected in IP Configuration. This function sets the virtual channel selection.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsiSs instance.
is the virtual channel selection value to be set.

briefdescription.para: This function will return the virtual channels selected.

detaileddescription.para: Get the virtual channel selection.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
Value of selected VCs.
In order to use dynamic VC selection, 'Allowed VC to all' must be selected in IP Configuration. This function returns the current virtual channel selection.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsiSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the short packets.

detaileddescription.para: Get short packet information.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
None.
None. This function retrieves the short packet information from the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsiSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the clk and data lane info.

detaileddescription.para: Get lane information.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
None.
None. This function retrieves the lane information from the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsiSs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the virtual channel information.

detaileddescription.para: Get virtual channel information.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
None.
None. This function retrieves the virtual channel information from the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsiSs instance.

briefdescription.para: Lookup the device configuration based on the base address.

detaileddescription.para: This function looks for the device configuration based on the unique device ID.
This function returns a reference to an XCsiSs_Config structure based on the base address. This function will return a NULL pointer if the base address is not found.

BaseAddress
is the base address of the device to lookup for.
XCsiSs_Config reference if BaseAddress is found, NULL otherwise.
None. The table XCsiSs_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration information for each instance of the device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the device being looked up

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration table entry corresponding to the given device ID, or NULL if no match is found

briefdescription.para: Get the driver index based on the base address.

detaileddescription.para: This function returns the Index number of config table using BaseAddress.
This function returns the driver index based on the base address.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is base address of the instance

simplesect.para: Index number of the config table

briefdescription.para: Run a self-test on the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: This function performs self test on MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem sub-cores.
This function runs a self-test on the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem driver/device. The self-test checks for proper functionality of the subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsiSs instance.
is a pointer to the XCsiSs core instance.

briefdescription.para: Interrupt handler for the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem.
This function is the interrupt handler for the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem driver. It processes all pending interrupts and routes them to the appropriate callback handlers.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XCsiSs instance (passed as void *).
None.
This function should be connected to the interrupt system. The application is responsible for connecting this function to the interrupt system. Application beyond this driver is also responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XCsiSs_SetCallBack() during initialization phase.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsiSs core instance that just interrupted.

briefdescription.para: Disable interrupts in the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: This function is used to disable the interrupts in the CSI core.
This function disables the specified interrupts in the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsiSs instance.
is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be disabled.
is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
Indicates Mask for enable interrupts.

briefdescription.para: Set callback function for interrupt handling.

detaileddescription.para: This routine installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:
This function sets a callback function for a specific interrupt handler type. The registered callback function will be called when the corresponding interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsiSs instance.

is the type of handler to register. It can be one of:
XCSISS_HANDLER_DPHY
XCSISS_HANDLER_PKTLVL
XCSISS_HANDLER_PROTLVL
XCSISS_HANDLER_SHORTPACKET
XCSISS_HANDLER_FRAMERECVD
XCSISS_HANDLER_OTHERERROR
XCSISS_HANDLER_VCX
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if callback was successfully registered.

para.preformatted: HandlerType Invoked by this driver when: ------------------------- -------------------------------------------------- XCSISS_HANDLER_DPHY A DPHY Level Error has been detected. XCSISS_HANDLER_PROTLVL A Protocol Level Error has been detected. XCSISS_HANDLER_PKTLVL A Packet Level Error has been detected. XCSISS_HANDLER_SHORTPACKET A Short packet has been received or the Short Packet FIFO is full. XCSISS_HANDLER_FRAMERECVD A Frame has been received XCSISS_HANDLER_OTHERERROR Any other type of interrupt has occurred like Stream Line Buffer Full, Incorrect Lanes, etc

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi instance to operate on
is the type of call back to be registered.
is the pointer to a call back function which is called when a particular event occurs.
is a void pointer to data to be referenced to by the CallBackFunc

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This function reads a value from a MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem register.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit read is performed. If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is read from the register. The most significant data will be read as 0.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the XCsiSs core instance.
is the register offset of the register (defined at the top of this file).

briefdescription.para: This function writes a value to a MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem register.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit write is performed. If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is written.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the XCsiSs core instance.
is the register offset of the register (defined at the top of this file) to be written.
is the 32-bit value to write into the register.

briefdescription.para: Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
This is main code of Xilinx MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem device driver.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains the implementation of the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem driver's static initialization functionality.
This file contains self test function for the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem.
This is the interrupt handling part of the Xilinx MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem device driver.
This header file contains identifiers and register-level core functions (or macros) that can be used to access the Xilinx MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem core.
This file contains the configuration table for the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem.
Please see xcsiss.h for more details of the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 vsa 07/21/15 Initial release 1.1 sss 08/17/16 Added 64 bit support sss 08/29/16 Added check for Dphy register interface

detaileddescription.para: This table contains configuration information for each MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem instance in the system. This file is automatically generated and should not be manually edited.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 vsa 07/25/15 Initial release

detaileddescription.para: For more information about the operation of this core see the hardware specification and documentation in the higher level driver xcsiss.h file.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 vsa 07/25/15 Initial release 1.1 sss 08/17/16 Added 64 bit support 1.2 vsa 03/12/17 Add Word Count corruption interrupt support 1.5 vsa 08/10/20 Add YUV 420 8bits support

detaileddescription.para: The interrupt registration and handler are defined here. The callbacks are registered for events which are interrupts clubbed together on the basis of the CSI specification. Refer to CSI driver for the event groups.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 vsa 07/27/15 Initial release

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 vsa 07/21/15 Initial release
